description: An AWS account — the billing and security boundary in Amazon Web Services.
An AWS geographic region — a cluster of data centers in a specific part of the world.
A negotiated access agreement between OneReach and a customer — defines what OneReach can do in a customer AWS account or PDE.
A GSX Account — the billing and access boundary within a PDE. Accounts have ACCESS_TO Spaces and are DEPLOYED_ON PDEs.
An AI agent or automated process with defined capabilities.
instructions: MERGE on id. Properties: name, description, categories (JSON array), keywords (JSON array), capabilities (JSON array), executionType, builtin (boolean), prompt, active. Connected to Library via CONTAINS. Enabled by Person via ENABLED.

description: A file, document, resource, or digital artifact managed in the graph.
crudExamples: {"create":"CREATE (a:Asset {id:$id, url:$url, created_by_app_name:$app, _history:'[]'}) RETURN a","list":"MATCH (:Ticket {id:$id})-[:HAS_ASSET]->(a) RETURN a"}

description: A specific skill or function that an Agent can perform.
A voice/video capture session — tracks WebRTC signaling state for real-time media capture.
Classification label for organizing tickets. Include provenance.
crudExamples: {"create":"MERGE (c:Category {id:$id}) SET c.name=$name, c.created_by_app_name=$app, c._history='[]' RETURN c","list":"MATCH (c:Category) RETURN c.id, c.name"}

description: A thematic grouping of related tags or concepts — clusters tags that belong together semantically.
An external customer entity — represents a customer organization or contact in the graph.
A data source connector — provides data to IDWs, Agents, and Flows.
instructions: Define type, description, endpoint. Types: keyvalue, file_storage, graph_traversal, api, mcp, websocket. Must include provenance fields per honor system.

description: A first-class documentation node in the graph — contains specs, access models, architecture decisions, or API references that other nodes link to via DOCUMENTS.
A discrete occurrence in the system — a trigger, signal, or state change that can initiate tasks or be recorded for audit.
crud_examples: {"by_correlation":"MATCH (e:Event {correlation_id:$cid}) OPTIONAL MATCH (e)-[:TRIGGERS]->(t:TaskItem) RETURN e,t ORDER BY e.emitted_at ASC LIMIT 100","recent":"MATCH (e:Event) RETURN e.id,e.name,e.source,e.status,e.emitted_at ORDER BY e.emitted_at DESC LIMIT 50"}

description: An asset type classifier for file-based content within a Space — links Space to its file Assets.
An Edison flow — a visual automation workflow with steps and connections.
An audit run from the Digital Twin Health Check — scores schema, data, temporal, and structural compliance.
instructions: Created by health-check.mjs. Query latest: MATCH (hc:HealthCheck) RETURN hc ORDER BY hc.run_at DESC LIMIT 1
crudExamples: {"list":"MATCH (hc:HealthCheck) RETURN hc ORDER BY hc.run_at DESC","trend":"MATCH (hc:HealthCheck) RETURN hc.run_at, hc.score_overall ORDER BY hc.run_at"}

description: An AWS IAM user or role with specific permissions in an AWS account.

name: IDW
label: IDW
description: An Intelligent Digital Worker — a marketplace agent with defined capabilities, deployed on a PDE.
A knowledge collection or knowledge graph that powers reasoning, retrieval, and semantic search.
instructions: KnowledgeModel nodes connect to Spaces via CONTAINS, to Agents via USES_KNOWLEDGE, and to Playbooks via INFORMS.

description: A catalog of all IDWs, Tools, and Agents available to an Organization — the marketplace container.
instructions: One Library per Organization via HAS_LIBRARY. Items linked via CONTAINS relationship to IDW, Tool, or Agent nodes. CONTAINS has addedAt and addedBy properties.

description: A person's authentication identity in GSX — the multi-user login that can switch between multiple UserAccounts across accounts and PDEs.
A note created from the OR-Mobile app — can carry additional labels like BasicNote for subtyping.
A grouping layer in the ontology — organizes tags into conceptual categories.
A top-level organization that owns a Library and has Teams of people.
instructions: MERGE on id. Properties: name, domain, plan (free|pro|enterprise), active. Relationships: HAS_TEAM to Team, HAS_LIBRARY to Library.

description: Platform Deployment Environment — a GSX installation in a specific AWS region/account. Can be OneReach-hosted or customer-hosted.
A person in the graph — employee, user, contact, or external collaborator.
instructions: MERGE on id (email). Set provenance fields on create/update per honor system.
crudExamples: {"create":"MERGE (p:Person {id:$email}) SET p.name=$name, p.created_by_app_name=$app, p.created_by_user=$user, p._history='[]' RETURN p","list":"MATCH (p:Person) RETURN p"}

description: An operational playbook — a reusable procedure, workflow, or runbook.
instructions: Create with provenance fields. Tickets join via: (t)-[:BELONGS_TO]->(pb)
crudExamples: {"create":"CREATE (pb:Playbook {id:$id, title:$title, created_by_app_name:$app, _history:'[]'}) RETURN pb","list":"MATCH (pb:Playbook) RETURN pb.id, pb.title","getTickets":"MATCH (t:Ticket)-[:BELONGS_TO]->(pb:Playbook {id:$id}) RETURN t"}
storage_details: Graph: metadata + provenance. KeyValue: content (riff:sheets). Spaces: synced items. IndexedDB: full local cache.

description: A shared system resource that tasks compete for — used for concurrency control and contention modeling in the task queue.
crud_examples: {"contention":"MATCH (res:Resource) OPTIONAL MATCH (res)-[:LOCKED_BY]->(holder:TaskItem) RETURN res.id,res.name,res.capacity,res.status,holder.id AS locked_by","waiters":"MATCH (w:TaskItem {status:'queued'})-[:REQUIRES]->(res:Resource)-[:LOCKED_BY]->(h:TaskItem {status:'running'}) RETURN res.id AS resource,h.id AS held_by,collect(w.id) AS waiting"}

description: A schema definition node — describes an entity type in the graph ontology.
instructions: HONOR SYSTEM: 1)Define APP_IDENTITY 2)On CREATE set provenance+_history=[] 3)On UPDATE set updated_by_* and append _history 4)SCHEMA-FIRST rule

description: An organizational container or workspace that groups related entities.
A taxonomy/ontology tag used to classify and organize entities.
A unit of work enqueued for processing — the executable item in a TaskQueue with dependency tracking and retry state.
crud_examples: {"by_status":"MATCH (t:TaskItem {status:'queued'})-[:ENQUEUED_IN]->(q:TaskQueue) RETURN t.id,t.name,t.priority,q.name AS queue ORDER BY t.priority DESC LIMIT 50","deps":"MATCH (t:TaskItem {id:$id}) OPTIONAL MATCH (t)-[:DEPENDS_ON_TASK]->(up:TaskItem) OPTIONAL MATCH (down:TaskItem)-[:DEPENDS_ON_TASK]->(t) RETURN t,collect(DISTINCT up) AS upstream,collect(DISTINCT down) AS downstream","causal":"MATCH (e:Event)-[:TRIGGERS]->(t:TaskItem {id:$id}) OPTIONAL MATCH (e)-[:PRODUCED_BY]->(src) RETURN e,src,t"}

description: A named queue that organizes and schedules TaskItems — acts as the dispatcher and backpressure boundary for a category of work.
crud_examples: {"runnable":"MATCH (t:TaskItem {status:'queued'})-[:ENQUEUED_IN]->(q:TaskQueue {status:'active'}) WHERE NOT (t)-[:DEPENDS_ON_TASK]->(:TaskItem {status:'queued'}) AND NOT (t)-[:DEPENDS_ON_TASK]->(:TaskItem {status:'running'}) AND NOT (t)-[:REQUIRES]->(:Resource)-[:LOCKED_BY]->(:TaskItem {status:'running'}) RETURN t,q ORDER BY t.priority DESC, t.queued_at ASC LIMIT 10","metrics":"MATCH (q:TaskQueue) OPTIONAL MATCH (t:TaskItem)-[:ENQUEUED_IN]->(q) RETURN q.id,q.name,q.status,count(t) AS total ORDER BY q.name","dead_letters":"MATCH (t:TaskItem {status:'dead_letter'})-[:ENQUEUED_IN]->(q:TaskQueue) RETURN t.id,t.name,t.error,q.id AS queue ORDER BY t.completed_at DESC LIMIT 50","subscribers":"MATCH (sub)-[:SUBSCRIBE_TO]->(q:TaskQueue) RETURN q.id,q.name,labels(sub) AS type,sub.id,sub.name"}

description: A group within an Organization — organizes Person nodes into functional teams.
instructions: MERGE on id. Connected to Organization via HAS_TEAM. Members connected via MEMBER relationship to Person nodes. MEMBER has role and joinedAt properties.

description: An asset type classifier for text-based content within a Space — links Space to its text Assets.
A support ticket, work item, or task that tracks an issue or request.
instructions: Create graph node with provenance, CREATED rel from Person, store content in Key/Value. Track changes in _history.
crudExamples: {"create":"CREATE (t:Ticket {id:$id, status:'OPEN', created_by_app_name:$app, _history:'[]'}) RETURN t","read":"MATCH (t:Ticket {id:$id}) RETURN t"}

description: A web tool (weblink) available in the GSX Desktop that can be opened in-app.
instructions: MERGE on id. Properties: name, url, description, docsUrl, windowSize, type, category, active. Connected to Library via CONTAINS. Enabled by Person via ENABLED.

description: A user identity scoped to a specific GSX Account — holds a userId, accountId, role, and user-scoped token. A person can have multiple UserAccounts in the same PDE under different GSX Accounts.
User preference profile — stores topic scores, signal counts, and preference clusters from Knowledge Twin.
instructions: MERGE on id (email). Link: (p:Person)-[:HAS_PREFERENCES]->(up:UserPreference). Graph stores summary (top subjects, distribution, signal count). Full signals stay in KV at ktwin:preferences.
crudExamples: { "create": "MERGE (up:UserPreference {id:$email}) SET up.signalCount=$count, up.topSubjects=$json, up.created_by_app_name=$app, up._history="[]" RETURN up", "read": "MATCH (p:Person {id:$email})-[:HAS_PREFERENCES]->(up) RETURN up", "update": "MATCH (up:UserPreference {id:$email}) SET up.signalCount=$count, up.topSubjects=$json, up.updated_by_app_name=$app RETURN up" }

description: An individual rule violation found during a Digital Twin Health Check — carries severity, fix Cypher, and links to the offending node.
instructions: Created by health-check.mjs. Fix: read fix_cypher and execute.

description: A volunteer activity record — tracks volunteer hours and status against tickets.
instructions: Create with provenance. On status change, update _history with old/new status.
crudExamples: {"create":"CREATE (vr:VolunteerRecord {id:$id, status:'PENDING', created_by_app_name:$app, _history:'[]'}) RETURN vr","accept":"MATCH (vr:VolunteerRecord {id:$id}) SET vr.status='ACCEPTED', vr.updated_by_app_name=$app RETURN vr"}
